proof_trace: When calloc succeeds but mutex_trylock fails (returns non-zero, i.e. lock held during hash expansion), the else branch at L313 executes `return NULL` without calling free(iter). The caller receives NULL and has no reference to the allocation, making it permanently unreachable. No other mechanism frees iter on this path.
attack_scenario: The LRU crawler thread calls assoc_get_iterator() periodically; if hash table expansion holds maintenance_lock, mutex_trylock fails and ~32 bytes leak per invocation. Repeated crawler cycles during sustained hash expansion lead to unbounded heap growth and eventual memory exhaustion.
reason: GT RA-M2-N6一致。assoc_get_iterator()でcalloc済みiterがmutex_trylock失敗時に未解放。PR#1217。

proof_trace: calloc allocates st.page_data at line 432 but there is no free(st.page_data) on any return path (the function has a single exit at line 463). Neither extstore_get_page_data nor extstore_evict_page have access to this pointer. Every call leaks page_count * sizeof(extstore_page_data) bytes of heap memory.
attack_scenario: An attacker sends repeated write requests that exhaust writable pages, causing extstore_write (lines 605, 620) to call _evict_page on each attempt. Each call leaks ~page_count*40 bytes; sustained traffic produces unbounded heap growth leading to OOM and service crash.

proof_trace: Two error return paths leak heap memory: at L421 (when `db = calloc(...)` returns NULL), `flist` is returned without being freed; at L428 (when `db->buf == NULL || db->fname == NULL`), both `flist` and `db` are returned without being freed. The sole `free(flist)` call is on the success path at L444 only, and no cleanup block exists before any error return.
attack_scenario: An attacker or low-memory condition that triggers `calloc`/`strdup` failure during proxy startup causes `flist` (and `db`) to leak; repeated restarts or OOM conditions at initialization will accumulate leaked memory, and the missing free before error return is a confirmed bug with no intentional design justification.
reason: GT RA-M2-N4一致。proxy_init_startfiles()でエラー時にflist/db等が未解放。PR#1215。

proof_trace: In the RESTART_NOTAG branch (L173-175), getline() has already allocated `line` (getline returned != -1), but `return RESTART_NOTAG` is reached without any `free(line)` call — the pointer is neither stored in `c->line` nor explicitly freed, confirming a heap leak. The EOF branch (L205-208) is not a practical leak for clean EOF since `line` starts as NULL and getline makes no allocation; it is only a theoretical concern on mid-stream read errors.
attack_scenario: A callback registered via restart_register calls restart_get_kv in a while(== RESTART_OK) loop; if the metadata file contains a T-line whose tag is not registered (e.g., written by a different server version), restart_get_kv returns RESTART_NOTAG and leaks the getline-allocated line buffer — the loop exits without abort, accumulating heap memory that is never reclaimed during the check phase.
reason: GT RA-M2-N5一致。restart_get_kv()でgetline確保のlineがエラー分岐で未解放。PR#1216。
